extra.message: User-controlled form['user_id'] is interpolated directly into a raw SQL query passed to User.objects.raw(), allowing SQL injection through the POSTed user_id value.

cwe: CWE-89: SQL Injection

extra.message: The ip and col values are formatted directly into a SELECT statement executed with cursor.execute(). app/views/admin/views.py passes request.GET['ip'] and query-string keys into this method, enabling SQL injection.

cwe: CWE-89: SQL Injection

extra.message: Uploaded file names are incorporated into a shell command without quoting or argument separation. A crafted filename can inject OS commands when backup=true triggers os.system().

cwe: CWE-78: OS Command Injection

extra.message: The download path is built from request.GET['name'] and opened directly. An authenticated attacker can use ../ sequences to read arbitrary files under paths reachable from BASE_DIR.

cwe: CWE-22: Path Traversal

extra.message: User-controlled POST field 'user' is base64-decoded and passed to pickle.loads(). Malicious pickle payloads can execute arbitrary code during password reset.

cwe: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

extra.message: The unauthenticated mobile API evaluates the user-controlled class query parameter with eval(), allowing arbitrary Python expression execution.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: The mobile object endpoint evaluates the user-controlled class query parameter with eval(), allowing arbitrary Python expression execution.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: The mobile API has no authentication and serializes model.objects.all() for a user-selected model class. Requesting class=User exposes all user records to unauthenticated clients.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: User.first_name is returned through mark_safe(), disabling Django escaping. Because first_name is accepted from signup/update forms and rendered in the logged-in navigation, this enables stored XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Cross-Site Scripting

extra.message: Admin dashboard routes require only any valid auth_token and never verify current_user.is_admin, so non-admin authenticated users can access admin functionality.

cwe: CWE-862: Missing Authorization

extra.message: The admin_delete_user endpoint is protected only by user_is_authenticated and deletes the selected user without checking administrator privileges.

cwe: CWE-862: Missing Authorization

extra.message: The admin_update_user endpoint lets any authenticated user update arbitrary users by selected_id because it never verifies that the caller is an administrator.

cwe: CWE-862: Missing Authorization

extra.message: The work_info view loads User.objects.get(id=user_id) from the URL and renders that user's work_info instead of enforcing that user_id matches the authenticated user, exposing other users' SSNs and employment data.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The pay deletion endpoint deletes Pay.objects.get(id=pay_id) without verifying the record belongs to the authenticated user, allowing IDOR deletion of other users' direct deposit records.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The message endpoint fetches and deletes Message by message_id only, without checking creator or receiver ownership, allowing authenticated users to read or delete other users' messages.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The login handler redirects to the POSTed path value without validating it is local, enabling open redirect attacks after authentication.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site

extra.message: Login failures return distinct messages for nonexistent email and incorrect password, allowing attackers to enumerate valid user email addresses.

cwe: CWE-203: Observable Discrepancy

extra.message: API token validation uses an unanchored regex search over a URL-decoded token. A token containing newline-delimited data can validate one user/hash while extrapolate_user reads a different leading user id, enabling authentication/authorization bypass.

cwe: CWE-287: Improper Authentication

extra.message: The API serializes User model instances directly and returns all fields, including password hashes, for admin and non-admin responses.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

extra.message: The user detail API serializes the User model directly, exposing sensitive fields such as password and auth_token in JSON responses.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

extra.message: Password reset tokens are deterministic user_id + MD5(email) values with no secret, expiration, or randomness. Anyone who knows a user's email and id can forge a reset token.

cwe: CWE-640: Weak Password Recovery Mechanism for Forgotten Password

extra.message: User passwords are hashed with a single unsalted MD5 digest instead of a password hashing algorithm such as bcrypt, scrypt, PBKDF2, or Argon2.

cwe: CWE-916: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort

extra.message: SSNs are stored in a plaintext CharField in addition to the encrypted_ssn field, exposing highly sensitive data if the database or model data is accessed.

cwe: CWE-312: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information

extra.message: Django SECRET_KEY is hardcoded in source. If this value is reused in deployment, attackers can forge signed data and compromise application security.

cwe: CWE-798: Use of Hard-coded Credentials

extra.message: The Docker database configuration hardcodes the root database password as 'password'.

cwe: CWE-798: Use of Hard-coded Credentials

extra.message: The AES encryption key used for sensitive values is hardcoded in settings.py, so disclosure of source code compromises all encrypted data.

cwe: CWE-321: Use of Hard-coded Cryptographic Key

extra.message: The auth_token cookie is set without httponly=True or secure=True, making the session token accessible to JavaScript and allowing transmission over plaintext HTTP.

cwe: CWE-1004: Sensitive Cookie Without HttpOnly Flag
CWE-614: Sensitive Cookie in HTTPS Session Without Secure Attribute

extra.message: Login sets the auth_token cookie without httponly=True or secure=True, exposing the authentication token to XSS theft and insecure transport.

cwe: CWE-1004: Sensitive Cookie Without HttpOnly Flag
CWE-614: Sensitive Cookie in HTTPS Session Without Secure Attribute
